relatedness_reasoning: The inbound file contains cash balance data from Accounts to Investments, while the outbound file contains detailed trade execution records from Investments to Risk & Compliance. These represent fundamentally different data domains: one is a cash position snapshot, the other is a collection of executed trade transactions. Only two fields (timestamp and department) have any semantic overlap, and the outbound file's rich trade-specific schema (trader_id, asset_class, instrument, ISIN, side, quantity, price, counterparty, etc.) has no corresponding sources in the inbound cash balance schema. The files do not form a plausible data lineage pipeline.
